differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus or other autoimmune cytopenia
next_steps: Bone marrow aspirate and biopsy with flow cytometry and cytogenetics
CT of the chest with intravenous contrast to evaluate for primary malignancy
Serum soluble IL-2 receptor and triglyceride levels to screen for HLH
Infectious disease workup (HIV, TB, fungal blood cultures)
Magnetic resonance imaging of the spine to evaluate for pathological fractures/masses
Interdisciplinary consultation with oncology and rheumatology

differential_diagnoses: Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH), likely secondary to occult malignancy
Occult solid tumor (e.g., small-cell lung cancer or pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor)
Acute pancreatitis, possibly secondary to pancreatic malignancy or hypertriglyceridemia
Systemic inflammatory response to occult metastatic disease
next_steps: Bone marrow aspirate and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and iron staining
CT of the chest with intravenous contrast to characterize lung nodules
Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the spine with contrast
Measurement of serum triglycerides, soluble IL-2 receptor, and fibrinogen (HLH-2004 criteria)
MRI/MRCP of the abdomen to further evaluate the pancreatic lesion and hepatic nodules
Calculate HScore to assess the probability of reactive hemophagocytic syndrome
